stimulus: Although most amphibians lay eggs in open water, some frog species lay eggs in foam nests built on land near water’s edge. Many herpetologists have assumed that foam nests would protect eggs from predators and thus would allow species that build them to occupy a wider variety of habitats than species that lay eggs in open water do. To evaluate this assumption, a research team led by biologist Carla Rodriguez analyzed data for over 2,500 frog species worldwide.
stem: Which finding from Rodriguez and her colleagues’ study, if true, would most directly challenge the assumption in the underlined sentence?

answerOptions.content: Species that lay eggs in open water tend to occupy a wider variety of habitats than species that build foam nests do.
Species that lay eggs in open water tend to have higher extinction rates than species that build foam nests.
Species that lay eggs in open water tend to be smaller in body size than species that build foam nests.
Species that lay eggs in open water tend to use fewer materials to build any structures than species that build foam nests do.

rationale: Choice A is the best answer because it directly contradicts the assumption that foam-nest builders should occupy a wider variety of habitats than open-water egg-layers — finding the opposite challenges the assumption. Choice B is incorrect because extinction rates aren’t the assumption’s subject (habitat variety is). Choice C is incorrect because body size doesn’t address habitat variety. Choice D is incorrect because the assumption concerns nest type, not materials used.
